title: What Does an MOT Test Check? Your Essential Guide to UK Car Inspections
metaDescription: Wondering what an MOT test checks in the UK? Discover every safety and environmental area inspected, from brakes to tyres, to keep your car legal and safe. Get prepared!
targetKeyword: what does an mot test check
content:
An MOT test checks your car's roadworthiness, ensuring it meets minimum safety and environmental standards set by the Driver and Vehicle Standards Agency (DVSA). It's an annual inspection for most UK vehicles over three years old, covering key areas like brakes, steering, lights, tyres, and emissions. Understanding these checks helps you keep your car safe and legal on British roads.

### What is an MOT Test, Really?

The MOT (Ministry of Transport) test is a vital annual safety check for vehicles over three years old in Great Britain. Its main goal isn't to be a full service, but to confirm your car meets legal requirements for safety and environmental protection. Think of it as a crucial health check for your vehicle's roadworthiness.

Without a valid MOT certificate, your car isn't legally allowed on the road. Driving without one can lead to hefty fines, points on your driving licence, and even invalidate your insurance policy. So, knowing **what does an MOT test check** is essential for every UK driver.

## Key Areas an MOT Test Checks on Your Car

An approved MOT test centre will thoroughly inspect many components of your vehicle. Here's a breakdown of the main areas:

### 1. Lights and Signalling Equipment

All your lights are checked for correct operation, colour, and alignment. This includes headlights, brake lights, indicators, fog lights, and even your number plate lights. They also inspect reflectors.

*   **Expert Tip:** Before your test, walk around your car. Ask a friend to check your brake lights while you press the pedal. It's a common MOT failure point that's easy to fix!

### 2. Steering and Suspension

Your car's steering and suspension are crucial for safe handling. Inspectors check the steering wheel, column, power steering system, suspension components, and shock absorbers for wear, damage, and secure fitting.

### 3. Brakes

The braking system is one of the most critical safety checks. They'll examine the condition and operation of your brake pedal, discs, pads, drums, hoses, and master cylinder. ABS warning lights are also scrutinised, along with overall braking efficiency.

### 4. Tyres and Wheels

Your tyres are your car's only contact with the road. Checks include tread depth (minimum 1.6mm across the central three-quarters of the tread), overall condition for cuts or bulges, correct size and type, and that wheels are securely fitted and undamaged.

*   **Expert Tip:** Check your tyre pressures and look for obvious damage regularly. Uneven tread wear can hint at suspension issues.

### 5. Seatbelts

All seatbelts fitted to your vehicle are checked for condition, operation, and the security of their anchorage points. They must retract properly and be free from cuts or fraying.

### 6. Bodywork, Structure and Chassis

The vehicle's main structure and chassis are inspected for significant rust, corrosion, damage, and sharp edges that could cause injury. Doors, the bonnet, and the boot must open and close securely.

### 7. Fuel System

The fuel system is checked for leaks, security of components, and the condition of the fuel cap. Any fuel leak poses a serious fire risk.

### 8. Exhaust System and Emissions

Your exhaust system must be secure, complete, and free from serious leaks. For modern vehicles, emissions are tested to ensure they meet environmental standards. Excessive smoke is an instant fail.

### 9. Driver's View & Controls

This covers windscreen condition (no chips or cracks in the driver's direct line of sight), wipers and washers (must clear the screen effectively), mirrors (secure and provide a clear view), and your horn (must work and be suitable).

### 10. Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) and Registration Plates

The VIN must be present, legible, and match your vehicle documents. Registration plates are checked for correct format, legibility, and secure fitting.

## Preparing Your Car for its MOT Test

While an MOT isn't a service, a little preparation can save you time and money. Simple checks you can do at home include:

*   **Lights:** Test all lights. Replace blown bulbs.
*   **Tyres:** Check tread depth and look for damage.
*   **Windscreen:** Ensure it's clean and free from major chips or cracks.
*   **Wipers:** Make sure they clear the screen properly.
*   **Fluid Levels:** Top up screenwash.
*   **Horn:** Give it a quick honk.

If you're unsure about your car's MOT status, use our handy [Free MOT Checker](/mot-checker) tool. For more detailed maintenance advice, browse our [Guides](/guides) section.

## MOT FAQs

### How long does an MOT test take?

An MOT test typically takes between 45 to 60 minutes. However, garages may require your car for longer to complete administrative tasks or carry out minor repairs if needed.

### What happens if my car fails its MOT?

If your car fails, you'll receive a 'Refusal of an MOT Test Certificate' form detailing the reasons. You usually have 10 working days to get the issues fixed and have a retest, often at a reduced or no fee, depending on the garage and timeframe. You cannot drive a car with a failed MOT unless you're taking it for repairs or to a pre-booked retest.

### Can I sell a car without an MOT?

Yes, you can sell a car without a valid MOT certificate. However, it's illegal to drive it on public roads without one (unless to a pre-booked MOT test). Many buyers prefer cars with a fresh MOT, so consider getting one before you sell. Or, if you're looking to upgrade, consider how easy it is to [sell your car](/sell) with BuyAndScrap.
